name: Rolled Stuffed Chicken with Hot Pepper Sauce
recipeIngredient: 1 whole chicken (about 1 pound), boned but left intact
1 large red bell pepper, chopped finely
1 large green bell pepper, chopped finely
1 teaspoon seeded and minced pickled jalapeno (adjust to taste)
recipeInstructions:
1. Cook the sausage in a skillet over moderate heat until no longer pink. Transfer to food processor, add egg, pulse for 30 seconds.
2. Cook spinach in water clinging to leaves until wilted. Drain, squeeze dry, and chop coarsely. Season with salt and pepper.
3. Heat olive oil in a skillet until hot but not smoking. Add garlic, cook stirring for 1 minute.
4. Spread cooked spinach mixture over chicken. Spread sausage evenly on top. Roll into log starting from the long side.
5. Roll skin around chicken to cover as much as possible. Tie at intervals with string and season with salt and pepper.
6. Heat oil in oven-proof skillet until hot but not smoking. Brown chicken seam-side down. Bake 350°F for 30 minutes, then rest covered for 10 minutes.
7. While baking sauce: Cook onion in butter until softened. Add bell peppers, cook covered stirring occasionally for 10 minutes.
8. Add stock and wine, bring to boil, simmer covered for 10 minutes. Add jalapeno, lemon juice, salt, and pepper, then puree mixture.
9. Sauce can be made ahead of time, kept covered chilled.
10. Remove strings from chicken, cut crosswise into slices. Serve with heated sauce over plates, arranging 3-4 slices per plate.
Yield: 4 servings
